subject: Databases
title: Deadlocks
description: A short lesson to help you apply Deadlocks.

hook.text: Transaction A locks row 1, waits for row 2. Transaction B locks row 2, waits for row 1. Neither can proceed. Deadlock.

buildup.text: Deadlocks occur when two transactions hold locks that the other needs. A circular dependency forms.

discovery.text: Databases detect deadlocks automatically. They pick a victim transaction and roll it back.

twist.text: Prevention: always acquire locks in the same order. If everyone locks row 1 first, then row 2, no deadlock.

climax.text: Monitor deadlock frequency in logs. Frequent deadlocks indicate a design problem, not bad luck.

punchline.text: Deadlocks: circular waits. Fix by locking in consistent order.

quiz.question: How do databases resolve deadlocks?
options: They detect the circular wait and automatically roll back one of the transactions
Deadlocks resolve themselves after a timeout
